easy.text: Describe the most beautiful gift you've ever received.
What is your favorite morning ritual?
Describe the texture and scent of a fresh orange.
If you could only eat one meal for the rest of your life, what would it be?
Explain why 'Early to bed, early to rise' is (or isn't) good advice.
Convince us that your favorite season is the best one.
Tell us about your first ever mobile phone.
Describe your favorite app without naming it.
Which is better for reading: a physical book or an e-reader?
Explain why everyone should know how to use a keyboard properly.
Tell us about a time you felt proud of a piece of work.
Explain the importance of taking short breaks during the day.
Convince a manager that a 'Coffee Monday' would improve morale.

medium.text: Describe a dream you had that felt incredibly real.
What does 'home' mean to you beyond a physical building?
Is it better to be a generalist who knows a little about everything, or a specialist?
Analyze the pros and cons of living in a big city vs. a small town.
Explain the concept of 'Grief' using the metaphor of the ocean.
If you could capture a rainbow in a jar, what would you do with it?
Describe the 'Internet' to someone from the year 1800.
How has the way we listen to music changed in the last 20 years?
Discuss the impact of social media on human friendship.
Persuade an audience to spend one full day a week without any screens.
You are giving a toast at a retirement party for a beloved colleague.
How should a leader handle a visible mistake they made?
Outline a plan to reduce unnecessary meetings in your office.
Pitch a new 'Flexible Hours' policy to a very traditional CEO.

hard.text: Describe a complex emotion (like nostalgia) without ever using the word itself.
Explain the 'Butterfly Effect' and its philosophical implications on free will.
Is privacy a fundamental human right or a luxury of the past?
Defend the idea that shadows are actually the most vibrant part of a room.
Convince an audience of aliens that humanity is worth saving.
Explain the concept of 'Artificial Intelligence' using only kitchen metaphors.
Defend the theory that we are becoming too dependent on our devices.
Discuss the ethical implications of self-driving cars making 'life or death' choices.
Will physical hardware ever become completely obsolete?
Deliver bad news about a major project delay to a high-stakes client.
Persuade a board of directors to pivot the company into an entirely new industry.
How will corporate culture evolve in a world where offices no longer exist?
Explain the long-term vision of a sustainable business to a skeptical investor.
